content.problemStatement:
Chef has a permutation†^{\dagger}† PPPof NNN integers. Consider an array SSS of MMM integers.

For each iii (1≤i≤M)(1 \leq i \leq M)(1≤i≤M), SiS_iSi​ is defined as

Si=Pi+Pi%M+1 S_i = P_i + P_{i\%M+1}Si​=Pi​+Pi%M+1​Chef forgot the original permutation PPP and only remembers array SSS. Help Chef by providing the number of all possible permutation PPP of size NNN which gives same array SSS.

Formally, given an array SSS of MMM positive integers, print the number of all possible permutation†^\dagger† PPP of size NNN such that

Si=Pi+Pi%M+1  for each  1≤i≤MS_i = P_i + P_{i\%M+1} \textbf{\ \ for each}\ \ 1\leq i \leq MSi​=Pi​+Pi%M+1​  for each  1≤i≤MAs the answer can be really large, print the number of all such permutations modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

†^\dagger† A permutation of size NNN is an array of size NNN such that each integer from 111 to NNN occurs exactly once in this array. For example, [3,1,2][3,1,2][3,1,2] is a permutation of size 333 but [2,1,4][2,1,4][2,1,4] or [1,2,2][1,2,2][1,2,2] are not permutation.


Input Format
The first line contains single integer TTT −-− the number of testcases. For each testcase,



the first line contains two space-separated integers N,MN,MN,M −-− the size of the permutation PPP and array SSS, respectively.
the second line contains MMM space-separated integers, S1,S2,⋯ ,SMS_1, S_2, \cdots, S_MS1​,S2​,⋯,SM​ −-− the elements of array SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of all such permutations modulo 109+710^9+7109+7
constraints: The sum of NNN over all testcases doesn't exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.
